briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the use of tmrctr PWM APIs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr driver Instance
is the base address of the XTmrCtr device.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Test is successful, otherwise XST_FAILURE
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function disconnects the interrupts for the Timer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr driver Instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing for the timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the callback function
is the number of the timer to which this handler is associated with.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the Tmrctr PWM example.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, else XST_FAILURE to indicate a Failure.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the timer counter driver and hardware device using interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: The example demonstrates the use of PWM feature of axi timers. PWM is configured to operate at specific duty cycle and after every N cycles the duty cycle is incremented until a specific duty cycle is achieved. No software validation of duty cycle is undergone in the example.
This example assumes that the interrupt controller is also present as a part of the system.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b cjp 03/28/18 First release 4.14 ml 07/14/25 Fixed GCC warnings. 4.15 bdk 12/08/25 Updated comments to support SDT flow for Doxygen documentation. 4.15 ml 02/03/26 Fix codespell warnings
